Overview.text:
Overview

Identify the activity, who is doing it, and when it will be reviewed.

What to do on this page

Use this page to identify the activity being assessed, where it happens, who is responsible for the assessment, and who could be affected.

1. Identify the task
Give enough context for someone else to understand the activity and location.
2. Set ownership
Record the assessor, dates and any SOP or reference numbers.
3. Confirm people at risk
Tick every group who may be exposed or affected by the task.
Business Unit
Location
Risk Assessor
*
SOP Ref number(s)
Date of Assessment
*
Date of Review
Activity Outline
Persons at Risk
*
Staff
Students
Third Party
Contractors
Visitors
Public
Go to Process Steps

Process Steps.text:
Process steps

Break the activity into repeatable steps, then add the chemicals used in each one. Chemical names in step text can be recognised automatically, and chemicals already added in earlier steps can be reused without re-entering their safety data.

Add step
What to do on this page

Use this page to break the job into practical steps, then record the chemicals, quantities, exposure routes, engineering controls and PPE for each step.

1. Add the step
Describe what happens in the order the task is carried out.
2. Add chemicals
Enter each chemical used in that step and complete its hazard and exposure details.
3. Record step controls
Select the engineering controls and PPE required before the step can be done safely.
1
Prepare and transfer mixed chemicals
15 chemicals
Complete
Drag steps to reorder
Go to Overview
Go to Non-Chemical Hazards

Non-Chemical Hazards.text:
Non-Chemical Hazards

Add and assess non-chemical hazards present in the workplace.

Add hazard
What to do on this page

Use this page to capture hazards from the task or workplace that are not caused by chemical exposure, such as equipment, movement, sharps, electrical risks or manual handling.

1. Decide if any apply
Add each relevant non-chemical hazard, or confirm that none apply.
2. Rate the risk
Score the initial risk before controls and the residual risk after controls.
3. Record actions
If more work is needed, add the owner, due date and completion date.
1
Glassware breakage and manual handling during chemical transfer
9 initial risk
Complete

Go to Controls

Controls.text:
Control measures

Apply the hierarchy of control. Click a section to add suggestions.

What to do on this page

Use this page to record the final control measures for the assessment. Start by checking whether the process-step engineering controls and PPE match the COSHH Essentials screening result, then complete the hierarchy of control fields below.

1. Check the screening
Use COSHH Essentials section to identify the recommended set of controls for the chemicals used.
2. Check step controls
A series of "Checks" are identified based off of comparison between controls you selected in the Process Steps and the COSHH essentials recommendations. Review each "Check", update any controls, and mark the "Checks" as complete as you go.
3. Record other controls
Review the other steps in the hierarchy of control, and indicate the other controls in place to ensure that work is safely completed. Check the prompts and suggestions for feedback on what to consider, alongside some standardised responses for you to consider.
COSHH Essentials screening
Click to review control recommendations
Approach 1
Approach 2
Approach 3
Approach 4 — Specialist advice required
4 approaches across 15 substances
Engineering and PPE by process step
0 of 2 checks complete
Engineering controls and PPE are recorded against each process step. Update them in Process Steps if the task controls change.
1. Prepare and transfer mixed chemicals
15 chemicals
Engineering
Fume hood
PPE
Gloves
Goggles
Lab coat
Face shield
Other
Use compatible secondary containment. Do not open incompatible chemicals together. Confirm glove material and any RPE need against SDS before work.
Checks for this step
Check controls for Phenol - Review SDS and ensure any specialist controls listed are documented in assessment. Add additional controls where required.
Check controls for Formaldehyde solution - Review SDS and ensure any specialist controls listed are documented in assessment. Add additional controls where required.
Hierarchy of control
1. Elimination / Substitution
Remove or replace with a less hazardous option.
Review elimination/substitution prompts
5 suggestions
2. Reduction
Reduce quantity, concentration or duration.
Review reduction prompts
5 suggestions
3. Administrative controls*
Change the way people work.
Review administrative prompts
8 suggestions
4. Air monitoring*
Confirm exposure stays controlled.
Review air monitoring prompts
4 suggestions
5. Health surveillance*
Record any OH decision or trigger.
Review health surveillance prompts
6 suggestions
Go to Non-Chemical Hazards
Go to Storage

Storage.text:
Storage

Suggestions below are derived from H-codes and GHS pictograms across the 15 chemicals you've added.

Storage guidance, not automatic approval. The classifications, cabinet layout and compatibility matrix are generated from the chemical data recorded in this assessment, including GHS hazards, physical state and PubChem-derived structure where available. They are starting points only and must be checked against SDS sections 7 and 10, local storage rules and competent H&S judgement before chemicals are stored.
What to do on this page

Use this page to review the suggested storage group for each chemical, check the SDS, update any asterisked fields, and confirm that the final storage recommendation is suitable.

1. Check each row
Compare the suggested group, guidance and segregation alert with SDS sections 7 and 10.
2. Update if needed
Change the storage group or text where the SDS or local rules require something different.
3. Confirm storage
Tick each chemical once you are satisfied the storage recommendation is correct.
Optional: Check Storage Layout: Review the storage layout against your storage in practice, and ensure that chemicals are correctly segregated as per the recommendations. Use the recommendations as a guide, and always confirm via the SDS.
Storage classification
Review and update chemical storage assignments before checking the supporting cabinet layout.
Chemical classification and storage assignment

Review each suggested group, amend it where needed, then tick Confirmed for every chemical before moving on.

Go to Controls

Emergency Response.text:
Emergency Response

Prompts and suggested wording are shown below, with hazard-specific suggestions derived from H-codes and GHS pictograms across the 15 chemicals you've added.

Suggestions, not recommendations. The chips below are generated from the GHS hazard codes and pictograms of the chemicals you have added. They are starting points only and must be reviewed against the substance Safety Data Sheet, local procedures and competent H&S judgement before being relied upon. CAT does not validate that the chosen controls are sufficient for your activity.
What to do on this page

Use this page to record practical emergency instructions for the task. Check the prompts, add any useful suggested wording, and amend it so it matches the SDS, local procedures, quantities and workplace arrangements.

1. Review prompts
Use the prompts to check credible exposure, spill, fire and waste scenarios for the chemicals and quantities used.
2. Add suggested wording
Use the suggested responses where helpful, then edit them so they reflect SDS requirements and local emergency arrangements.
3. Make it actionable
Record what people must do, what equipment is needed, who to contact and when the situation must be escalated.
Emergency response
Review prompts, add suggested wording, then tailor each response to the SDS and local procedure.
1. First aid*
Exposure response and medical escalation
First-aid prompts
First aid*
6 suggestions
2. Spills*
Spill control, isolation and clean-up
Spill-response prompts
Spills*
5 suggestions
3. Fire response*
Fire, reaction and emergency responder information
Fire-response prompts
Fire*
5 suggestions
4. Waste and disposal*
Waste streams, segregation and collection route
Waste prompts
Waste and disposal*
10 suggestions
Other emergency arrangements
(optional)
Other arrangements
Go to Storage
Go to Briefing & Sign-off

Briefing & Sign-off.text:
Briefing Record

Each worker confirms they have been briefed on this assessment.

Bulk add
Add worker
What to do on this page

Use this page to record who has been briefed on the completed assessment before they carry out the work.

1. Add workers
Add everyone who needs to understand and follow this assessment.
2. Brief the assessment
Make sure they understand the task, hazards, controls, emergency actions and limits.
3. Record sign-off
Capture the name, date and signature or acknowledgement for each person.
ENTRY 1
Name of worker *
Date
Add signature (optional)
Go to Emergency Response
Go to Complete & Export

Complete & Export.text:
Complete & Export

Export the finished COSHH assessment as PDF or DOCX.

What to do on this page

Use this page as the final check before downloading the assessment. Export is available once the required sections are complete.

1. Resolve pending sections
Use the list below to see what still needs attention before export unlocks.
2. Export the file
Download a PDF or DOCX copy for your records, briefing pack or approval route.
3. Keep a backup
Use the catdraft controls in the top bar if you need to restore or continue the draft later.
Export PDF
Export DOCX
Files download to your device. Nothing is uploaded. Use the catdraft controls in the top bar for draft backup and restore.
Go to Briefing & Sign-off
